root_text.sn29.21-50:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn29.21-50:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā jalābujānaṁ nāgānaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn29.21-50:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekacco kāyena dvayakārī hoti, vācāya dvayakārī, manasā dvayakārī.
root_text.sn29.21-50:2.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā opapātikānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyyan’ti.
root_text.sn29.21-50:2.9: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā opapātikānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn29.21-50:2.10: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā opapātikānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti.
root_text.sn29.21-50:3.2: Evaṁ catūsu yonīsu cattālīsaṁ veyyākaraṇā honti.
root_text.sn29.21-50:3.3: Purimehi pana dasahi suttantehi saha honti paṇṇāsasuttantāti.)

translation_text.sn29.21-50:0.3: Thirty Discourses On How Giving Helps to Become Womb-Born, Etc.
translation_text.sn29.21-50:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn29.21-50:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the womb-born dragons …
translation_text.sn29.21-50:2.1: “Mendicant, it’s when someone does both kinds of deeds by body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn29.21-50:2.3: ‘The spontaneously-born dragons are long-lived, beautiful, and very happy.’
translation_text.sn29.21-50:2.5: ‘If only, when my body breaks up, after death, I would be reborn in the company of the spontaneously-born dragons!’
translation_text.sn29.21-50:2.9: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the spontaneously-born dragons.
translation_text.sn29.21-50:2.10: This is the cause, this is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the spontaneously-born dragons.”
translation_text.sn29.21-50:3.1: (Each set of ten discourses of this series should be told in full the same way.)
translation_text.sn29.21-50:4.7: The Linked Discourses on dragons are complete.
